title: Government and opposition postpone reduction of seats to prioritize reform of the Imperial Household Law
summary: The ruling party and the Innovation Party agree to postpone the project of reducing parliamentary seats and focus on revising the imperial succession law.

title: Three former NGO executives arrested for illegal kidney trafficking in Cambodia
summary: Japanese police arrest three former executives of a non-profit organization accused of brokering kidney transplants in exchange for large sums of money.

title: Fuji TV publicly apologizes for failing to ensure a safe environment on a program
summary: The Japanese network acknowledges its responsibility following allegations of harassment between actors Satō Jirō and Hashimoto Ai.

title: Former nursing home employee sentenced to 20 years in prison for killing two residents
summary: The Mito court sentences him to 20 years for murder, though acquits him of one of the two charges due to lack of evidence.

title: French justice confirms conviction of Marine Le Pen for embezzlement of funds
summary: The appeals court upholds the sentence that could prevent the far-right leader from running in the 2027 presidential elections.

title: Shizuoka governor gives green light for start of maglev train construction
summary: Construction of the maglev line, stalled by local opposition, moves forward towards its opening after agreement on the water of the Ōi River.

title: Trump criticizes FIFA for sanctioning American player in unplayed match
summary: The former US president calls the red card to Folarin Balogun unfair and says it was only an accidental collision between two players.

title: Debates on reform of the Imperial Household Law advance without agreement on succession
summary: The ruling party rejects live streaming of hearings and the government avoids commenting on inheritance rights of adopted children.

title: Japanese ships cross the Strait of Hormuz as tension grows over attack attributed to Iran
summary: Thirteen ships with Japanese flag cross the area after an attack on a merchant vessel that the US links to Tehran.

title: Day of professional baseball in Japan with comebacks and outstanding performances
summary: The Tokyo Giants beat the Hanshin Tigers with a double by Sakamoto, while the Hiroshima Carp win with a home run by Sakakura and DeNA achieves a no-hitter.

title: Trump attends NATO summit and lashes out at Italian prime minister
summary: The former US president demands a 'restraining order' for Giorgia Meloni and criticizes lack of defense spending by allies.

title: Mbappé calls Paraguayan senator who made racist comments 'despicable'
summary: The French footballer receives support from the French president after the insults posted on social media by the South American politician.

title: Several bombs explode in Damascus during President Macron's visit
summary: At least 18 police are injured in explosions that authorities consider an attempted attack against the French president, who was unharmed.

title: A 25-year-old man arrested for hitting and killing a woman in Osaka
summary: Police arrest the alleged responsible party for the accident in which a cyclist died in the town of Sennan.

title: Shohei Ohtani is one home run away from reaching 300 home runs in Major League Baseball
summary: The Japanese hits his nineteenth homer of the season and becomes the fastest player in history to reach that figure.

title: Kana Fukuma earns the right to challenge Koki Nishiyama for the women's shogi title
summary: The five-time women's champion will face the current champion, who aims to become the first woman to obtain the rank of professional player.

title: China successfully launches ballistic missile from a submarine
summary: Beijing says the test strengthens its nuclear deterrence capability and rejects US criticism of its weapons policy.

title: Former Fukuoka assemblyman says party demanded bribes of 20 million yen
summary: The former president of the provincial assembly claims that LDP leaders asked him for illegal payments to avoid being sidelined in the party.

title: Japan's southernmost ski resort closes due to lack of snow and profitability
summary: The municipality of Gokase decides to end operation of the slope, affected by global warming and a drop in the number of visitors.

title: Business bankruptcies increase 37% in the Hokuriku region and a payment firm goes bankrupt
summary: The rise in material costs causes an increase in payment suspensions, while the card processing company Zentohshin declares insolvency.
